one_line: Emperor of Vijayanagara leading the Raichur campaign at the zenith of the Tuluva dynasty.

appearance.age_look: Late 30s, athletic and broad-shouldered, with a disciplined warrior’s build.
appearance.face: Oval face with high cheekbones, medium-brown South Indian complexion, sharp nose, neatly trimmed black moustache, calm intense eyes, default expression a composed, confident half-smile that suggests strategic focus.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair tied in a compact low knot at the back of the head, front kept smooth with a slight center part; adorned with a tall gold kuntala-style turban crown wrapped in deep maroon cloth, a central lotus-shaped gold ornament, and a single small emerald at the forehead.
appearance.costume: Era-accurate early 16th-century Vijayanagara royal war attire: a fitted, long-sleeved jamā-style upper garment in rich deep maroon silk with subtle gold floral brocade, reaching mid-thigh; over it a sleeveless quilted cotton war-vest dyed muted olive green with faint block-printed geometric borders, suggesting light armor without metal fantasy plating; a wide, elaborately worked gold belt with repoussé motifs cinching the waist and securing a cream-colored cotton angavastra sash draped from left shoulder across chest and falling behind; lower body in tightly wrapped, ankle-length churidar-style trousers in dark indigo cotton, with fine self-stripes and slight gathering at the ankles; simple but sturdy brown leather boots reaching just above the ankle, era-plausible for campaign riding; jewelry limited to a pair of thick gold karnaphool earrings, a short gold necklace with a small ruby pendant, and a few gold rings on the right hand, overall dignified and practical for a battlefield king.
appearance.signature: A straight-backed Deccan-style talwar sword with a gently curving steel blade, disc pommel and knuckle guard, fitted with a gold hilt inlaid with small rubies, carried in a dark brown leather scabbard with gold fittings and usually held in his right hand or resting at his left hip.

ref_desc: Krishnadevaraya appears as a late-30s, medium-brown, broad-shouldered South Indian king with a maroon gold-crowned turban, maroon silk jamā, olive war-vest, indigo churidar, cream sash, gold belt and jewelry, wielding a ruby-hilted talwar in a brown scabbard with a deep maroon, olive, indigo, cream and gold palette.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Krishnadevaraya on campaign: a late-30s, broad-shouldered South Indian emperor with an oval, medium-brown face, sharp nose, neat black moustache and calm intense eyes, wearing thick black hair tied in a compact low knot beneath a tall gold kuntala-like turban crown wrapped in deep maroon cloth with a central lotus ornament and small emerald. Show him in early 16th-century Vijayanagara war attire—a fitted long-sleeved deep maroon silk jamā with subtle gold floral brocade under a sleeveless muted olive green quilted war-vest, a wide ornate gold belt at the waist securing a cream cotton angavastra sash draped from left shoulder across the chest, dark indigo churidar-style trousers gathered at the ankles, and sturdy brown leather ankle boots. Add dignified gold jewelry: thick gold earrings, a short gold necklace with ruby pendant, and a few gold rings. His signature weapon is a Deccan talwar with gently curving steel blade, gold disc-pommel hilt inlaid with rubies, and a dark brown leather scabbard with gold fittings at his left hip or held in his right hand. Use a deep maroon, olive green, dark indigo, cream and gold color scheme, and present him front-facing with clear head-to-toe costume details suitable for consistent redrawing in multiple panels.
